Requirement: WHILE VehSpd is within [0 kph, 250 kph], THE SYSTEM SHALL process the VehSpd value within [50 ms].
Rationale: The requirement uses a generic system name 'THE SYSTEM' instead of specifying the exact system/component responsible for processing VehSpd. INCOSE guidelines require clear identification of the system or component to ensure accountability and testability.

Requirement: IF VehSpd > 250 kph, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_OOR_HI_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_OOR_HI - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) under a single THEN clause, violating EARS syntax which mandates exactly one system response per requirement. INCOSE guidelines also recommend atomic requirements to ensure testability and traceability.

Requirement: IF VehSpd < 0 kph, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_OOR_LO_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_OOR_LO - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) combined into a single statement. INCOSE guidelines and EARS syntax recommend atomic requirements with one distinct system response per requirement to ensure testability, traceability, and unambiguous verification.

Requirement: IF VehSpd is not received within [50 ms], THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_TO_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_TO - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement explicitly defines a trigger (VehSpd not received within 50 ms), measurable conditions (time thresholds), and specific system responses (detection, reaction, DTC setting, state transition) with quantifiable timing constraints. It adheres to INCOSE guidelines for clarity, measurability, and testability, and follows EARS syntax by structuring the requirement as a conditional response to a defined event.
Source: Incomplete Specification for LED Blinking Behavior (Page 9) [Score: 0.51]

Requirement: IF VehSpd AliveCounter is unchanged for [3 consecutive cycles], THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_ALC_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_ALC - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement uses 'THE SYSTEM' as a generic system name without explicitly identifying the specific system/component responsible for the response. INCOSE guidelines and EARS syntax require clear system identification to avoid ambiguity in implementation and verification.
Source: Incomplete Specification for LED Blinking Behavior (Page 9) [Score: 0.51]

Requirement: IF VehSpd CRC is invalid, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_CRC_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_CRC - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple distinct system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) combined into a single statement. INCOSE guidelines require requirements to be atomic (one requirement per line of text) to ensure testability and traceability. Each action should be a separate requirement to maintain clarity and verifiability.
